perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence in accordance with this Agreement; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information (but in no event less than reasonable care); (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, independent contractors and consultants) without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party except as expressly permitted under Paragraph 4; and (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence in accordance with this Agreement, except for information already known to the Receiving Party; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information (but in no event less than reasonable care); (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, independent contractors and consultants) without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party except as expressly permitted under Paragraph 4; and (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text **contradicts the obligation of strict confidence** by introducing an exception for information already known to the Receiving Party. This creates an **in-text contradiction** with the original intention of protecting all Confidential Information, regardless of prior knowledge. The change **undermines the enforceability** of the confidentiality obligation, as the Receiving Party could argue that certain information was already known and therefore not subject to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees: (a) to hold the other Party’s Confidential Information in strict confidence in accordance with this Agreement, except for information already known to the Receiving Party; (b) to exercise at least the same care in protecting the other Party’s Confidential Information from disclosure as the Party uses with regard to its own Confidential Information (but in no event less than reasonable care); (c) not to disclose such Confidential Information to third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, independent contractors and consultants) without the prior, written consent of the Disclosing Party except as expressly permitted under Paragraph 4; and (d) not to use any Confidential Information for any purpose except for the Business Purpose without the prior written consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.justification: The modified text introduces an exception to the 'strict confidence' obligation by allowing disclosure of information already known to the Receiving Party. This directly contradicts the original intent to protect *all* Confidential Information, regardless of prior knowledge. The changed text, as per the Perturbation Explanation, creates an in-text contradiction with the obligation of strict confidence, undermining the enforceability of the confidentiality obligation, thus creating a strong contradiction. The Contradicted Text is identical to the Changed text, but this emphasizes the modification of the original text has created a contradiction by being in two places at once.

perturbation.original_text: Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each Party agrees to instruct all such Representatives (i) of the identities of the Party’s other permitted Representatives, and (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, subsidiaries, independent contractors, and consultants) other than the identified Representatives without the prior written permission of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each Party agrees to instruct all such Representatives (i) of the identities of the Party’s other permitted Representatives, and (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, subsidiaries, independent contractors, and consultants) other than the identified Representatives without the prior written permission of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall *not* be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text **directly contradicts** the original statement that 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement' by stating that they 'shall *not* be responsible'. This **in-text contradiction** introduces ambiguity about who bears the liability if a Representative breaches the confidentiality agreement. This contradiction **creates legal uncertainty**.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Subject to the restrictions set forth below in this Section 4, each Party may disclose the other Party’s Confidential Information to its directors, officers, employees, legal advisors, financial advisors and accountants, each bound by obligations of confidentiality CONFIDENTIAL Page 2 of 7 consistent with this Agreement, who have a bona fide need to know (“Representatives”), but only to the extent necessary to carry out the Business Purpose. Each Party agrees to instruct all such Representatives (i) of the identities of the Party’s other permitted Representatives, and (ii) not to disclose such Confidential Information to any third parties (including, without limitation, any clients, affiliates, subsidiaries, independent contractors, and consultants) other than the identified Representatives without the prior written permission of the Disclosing Party. The Receiving Party shall *not* be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'The Receiving Party shall *not* be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement,' while the contradicted text states that 'The Receiving Party shall be responsible for the conduct and compliance of its Representatives under this Agreement.' This is a direct and clear contradiction regarding the responsibility of the Receiving Party, making the agreement ambiguous and potentially unenforceable. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date, and that no copies of the removed materials or items are to be made without the consent of the Disclosing Party.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date. Copies of the removed materials or items may be made for internal review purposes, regardless of the Disclosing Party's consent.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text **introduces a contradiction** regarding the making of copies of removed confidential materials. Originally, no copies could be made without the Disclosing Party's consent. Now, copies can be made for internal review, regardless of consent. This **creates uncertainty** and could lead to disputes about the scope of permitted use of the confidential information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party agrees not to remove any materials or tangible items containing any of the other Party’s Confidential Information from the premises of the Disclosing Party without the Disclosing Party’s consent. Each Party agrees to comply with any and all terms and conditions that the Disclosing Party imposes upon approved removal of such materials or items, including, without limitation, that the removed materials or items must be returned by a certain date. Copies of the removed materials or items may be made for internal review purposes, regardless of the Disclosing Party's consent.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text and the contradicted text. The original text states that copies of removed materials cannot be made without the Disclosing Party's consent. The changed text explicitly allows copies to be made for internal review, regardless of the Disclosing Party's consent. The contradicted text section is the exact same as the Changed Text, which indicates that there is a contradiction. This creates a clear contradiction within the contract, making it an in-text contradiction as defined.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered or supplemented except by a writing signed by both Parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered or supplemented except by a writing signed by the Party seeking the amendment.
perturbation.explanation: The modified text **contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement** to amend the contract. The original clause stipulates that any amendment must be signed by *both* parties. The change allows one party to unilaterally amend the agreement with just their signature. This **creates an in-text contradiction** and significantly alters the power dynamic and enforceability of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Amendment; Modification . This Agreement may not be amended, modified, altered or supplemented except by a writing signed by the Party seeking the amendment.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original text such that it now allows a single party to amend the agreement, while the original text and the contradicted text require both parties to sign the amendment. This directly contradicts the requirement for mutual agreement, creating a strong in-text contradiction.
